description: Colombian plebiscite on the FARC peace agreement signed in Cartagena. NO won by razor-thin margin 50.2% vs YES 49.8% with extremely low turnout of 37.4%. Tests narrow upset with low engagement and regional polarization.

key_events.description: Santos and FARC leader Timochenko sign peace deal in Cartagena ceremony. Massive international praise but domestic skepticism lingers.
Uribe's Centro Democrático launches aggressive NO campaign. Frames deal as impunity for FARC crimes and threat to traditional values.
Evangelical churches mobilize against deal, linking it to 'gender ideology' provisions in the agreement text.
Polls consistently show YES ahead by 10-20 points. YES campaign becomes complacent, NO intensifies ground game.
Hurricane Matthew hits Caribbean coast (pro-YES regions), depressing turnout in Barranquilla, Cartagena, and Santa Marta on vote day.

calibration_notes: Tests massive polling failure and upset result. Polls showed YES leading by 10-20+ points but NO won by 0.4%. Key dynamics: extremely low turnout (37.4%) with differential mobilization favoring NO, hurricane suppressing YES-leaning coastal vote, complacency effect on YES supporters, emotional NO campaign (justice for victims, anti-impunity) vs rational YES campaign (peace dividend), evangelical mobilization as unexpected force multiplier for NO. Ultimate stress test for models handling shy/hidden opposition and turnout asymmetry.
